steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the most logical transition between two sentences. The first sentence makes a general claim: mushrooms contain phenolic compounds such as protocatechuic acid and biochanin. The second sentence then gives specific instances of Ferreira detecting those exact compounds in mushrooms. This is a general statement followed by an illustrative example, so the transition must signal illustration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Nevertheless,': Signals a concession or contrast. There is no contrast between the two sentences. ✗
  • Choice B — 'However,': Also signals contrast. The second sentence supports and illustrates the first, not contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'For this reason,': Signals that the second sentence is a result or consequence of the first. Ferreira's detections are not caused by the claim; they illustrate it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'For example,': Signals that what follows illustrates the preceding claim. Ferreira detecting protocatechuic acid in specific mushroom species is a concrete example of the general statement that mushrooms contain phenolic compounds. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
